timeout.description: Max seconds the runner lets a single invocation run before killing it and marking it failed. Defaults to 600. Raise it for long scanners (e.g. SSL Labs).

aggregation.description: How fanout outputs combine. per_target = one envelope per target invocation; aggregate = one combined envelope from a single invocation that receives the whole target list. Only meaningful when supports_targets is true.

per_target.description: If true, this secret is provided per-target during fanout (different value per target invocation). Only meaningful when supports_targets is true.

config_schema.description: Non-secret config knobs this fetcher accepts. Each value declares a field; when it has an `env`, the runner injects the resolved value as that env var. Platform-wide config lives in fetchers/_categories/<category>.yaml instead.

target_schema.description: When supports_targets is true, describes the shape of each target entry the runner will pass in. Free-form for v0.x; the runner will formalize validation when it lands.

evidence_set.description: Paramify evidence-set identity for this fetcher (1 fetcher = 1 evidence set). Fetcher-knowledge — what this evidence is and how it's collected — so it ships with the fetcher and is the shipped default the runner carries into the envelope. Customers override reference_id per program in the uploader config; they never edit this. Do NOT add controls/solution_capabilities here — that linkage stays Paramify-side. See docs/uploader_design.md.

reference_id.description: Stable idempotency key the uploader get-or-creates the evidence set by (e.g. EVD-OKTA-PHISHING-MFA).

instructions.description: How this evidence is collected (e.g. the API calls / commands). Written once into the evidence set on create.

description.description: Evidence-set description. Defaults to the fetcher's top-level description when omitted.

ksis.description: FedRAMP 20x Key Security Indicators this fetcher's evidence speaks to (1 or more). Intrinsic to the fetcher, not a per-customer control mapping (those stay Paramify-side).

validators.description: Regex checks over this fetcher's evidence that show the control is being implemented (collection-side assertions, not per-customer compliance judgment). Each regex is matched against the fetcher's evidence payload.
